test_cases.title: Valid user sees inventory after login
test_cases.description: Happy path: a registered, non-locked user signs in with correct credentials and reaches the inventory list.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC is about the user journey through the UI — they submit the form and land on the inventory page. The journey crosses page boundaries; an API check alone would not validate that the redirect and inventory render correctly.
preconditions: A non-locked user account exists in the test environment.
BASE_URL points at the login page.
expected_results: URL changes to the inventory page.
At least one inventory item is visible on the page.

test_cases.title: Invalid password is rejected with a clear error
test_cases.description: Negative case: the user submits a wrong password. The form rejects the credentials and shows an error.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC explicitly says the user remains on the login page AND sees an error message. The 'sees an error' part is a UI assertion and belongs at E2E. The 'no partial session' part is also covered by API but is asserted here too because the user-facing behaviour is what the AC names.
preconditions: A non-locked user account exists in the test environment.

steps.action: Fill the password field with a wrong value.

expected_results: URL remains on the login page.
An error message is visible explaining the credentials were not accepted.

test_cases.title: Locked account is rejected at the API boundary
test_cases.description: Permission check: when a locked account submits valid credentials, the auth endpoint must refuse the request and not create a session. The UI's error display is covered by a separate E2E sibling case if needed; this case asserts the server-side guarantee.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC is about session creation, not visual feedback. Permission/role checks belong at the API per docs/automation-decision-model.md; testing only via UI would let an API-level bypass slip through. The Phase 1.5 API Agent will pick this case up from the test-cases JSON.
preconditions: A locked user account exists in the test environment.

steps.action: POST credentials for the locked account to the auth endpoint.

expected_results: Response status code is in the 4xx range (typically 401 or 403).
Response body does not contain a session token.
No session cookie is set on the response.

test_cases.title: Visual review of login error styling
test_cases.description: Subjective check that the error banner is legible and on-brand. Captured for manual execution; not automated.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The check is about visual quality — colour, contrast, copy readability — which automated assertions cannot judge reliably. Marked manual so it still appears on the QA checklist but does not bloat the automation suite.

steps.action: Trigger an invalid-credentials error and inspect the banner visually.

expected_results: The banner uses an error colour and is readable against the page background.
The copy is specific enough that a non-technical user knows what to do next.
